item19.economics_label: derived pre-rent operating income (NOT net income; NOT profit; NOT EBITDA)

most_material: Crawford + McPherson (77 Black franchisees race discrimination)
Michell (CT franchisee discrimination, partial dismissal Sep 2025)
Williams (E. coli Quarter Pounder class action, Oct 2024)
Le (McRib deceptive marketing class action, Dec 2025)

kill_switches_summary: No right to renew or extend (renewal is discretionary franchisor policy)
No exclusive or protected territory; encroachment permitted
Full-time personal operation required (no passive ownership)
Franchisor holds uncapped independent access to transaction-level POS data
Mandatory Sesame POS + Cashless + Gift Card System
Real estate control: franchisee must lease premises from franchisor; default on rent = termination trigger
risk_flags: 113 franchisees departed in the most recent fiscal year
Multiple discrimination lawsuits by current/former franchisees
9 of 31 McOpCo 2024 sales exceeded the Item 7 high-end by up to $2.04M
Item 19 pro forma excludes rent, royalty, D&A, interest, taxes — real-world net can be dramatically lower

unresolveds.description: Exhibit A cash flow statement + notes were decoded in A2 depth pass; operating $4,552.4M, investing $(1,060.7)M, financing $(3,499.6)M with full dividend-to-parent upstream
Exhibits B/C/D (Franchise Agreement forms) not independently parsed beyond Item 17 summaries
Exhibit S departed franchisee identities not captured; only count (113)
Item 20 Table 3 state-level franchised status rows not normalized; totals captured
Item 2 officer start dates and biographical footnotes not normalized

BoA guarantee-program loan loss/default rates and franchisee uptake of the BoA loan program are not disclosed in the FDD. Item 10 describes terms and pricing but provides no visibility into the realized default or utilization experience of franchisees using the program.
unresolveds.source: Item 10 (page 30); carried forward from runs/mcdonalds-2025-merged U-4

contradictions.description: Item 19 pro forma sample described as both '11,332 independent franchisee traditional restaurants' and '11,322 independent franchisee traditional restaurants' in adjacent paragraphs. Likely a typo in source document.

Item 7 published traditional range maxes at $2,728,000 but Item 7 Note 11 discloses that 9 of 31 McOpCo sales in 2024 exceeded the high end by up to $2,035,816 — published range understates upper-half McOpCo acquisition cost.

Retained earnings unchanged at $4,633.9M across FY2022-FY2024 despite $3.4B+ of annual net income. Explained in A2 by the 100% upstream dividend policy disclosed in Exhibit A members' equity statement and related party transactions note.
Item 20 Table 1 shows Franchised start 2022 = 12,775 but the Exhibit A Nature of Business note shows Franchised 2022 = 12,751. Two different sources report slightly different counts; not explained.

Item 19 pro forma is derived from a restaurant cohort whose royalty during the covered period was 4% of Gross Sales. As of January 1, 2024, new restaurants, McOpCo-sale purchases, and franchisor ROFR resales pay 5%. The pro forma therefore understates the royalty burden for new franchisees by 1 percentage point of Gross Sales — at $3.4M sales, roughly $34,000 less OIBO than the table presents.

Item 20 Table 1 reports 12,887 franchised outlets at year-end 2024. Exhibit A Nature of Business note reports 12,886 franchised outlets at year-end 2024. One-outlet gap. Both sources are from the same filing.

hawaii.focus: Item 17 anti-waiver language + HI filing disclosure

maryland.focus: Release exclusion for MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law; MD venue option; 3-year limitations period

minnesota.focus: Minn. Stat. 80C.14 termination notice/cure (90d/60d cure, 180d non-renewal), reasonable transfer consent, trademark defense

north_dakota.focus: Non-compete generally unenforceable; ND law governs ND Franchise Investment Law claims

washington.focus: RCW 19.100.180 / WA FIPA supremacy; separate AOD removes no-hire/no-poach provisions

item21_financials_depth.dividend_policy: 100% upstream of net income to parent each year
item21_financials_depth.subsequent_event: $2.4B parent payable converted to equity on 2025-02-17

item1_structural_depth.source: Exhibit A Nature of Business note (page 63)
